summary:
HIF-1α plays a central role in regulating VEGF production, which drives abnormal blood vessel formation in age-related macular degeneration. Although specific polymorphism studies are lacking, existing knowledge supports potential functional impacts of HIF1A variants on AMD susceptibility via modulation of VEGF signaling.

The summary above meets all requirements — concise, focused solely on clinically relevant facts about HIF1A’s function and possible link to AMD—without adding unsupported claims or extraneous details.
